_meta.description: BEARISH counterpart to properties-collier-value.sample.json — polarity lock for properties-collier-value. Engineered so the current complete year (year-1 relative to a 2026 run = 2025) sits WELL BELOW the trailing 3yr baseline (2022/2023/2024), forcing the homes-sold z-score negative -> brain direction 'bearish'. months_of_supply simultaneously climbs to 8.5 (>= 7.5) — a glutted, buyer-favorable market. If a polarity/threshold inversion ever made this collapsing market read 'bullish', the bearish test turns red. The bullish fixture is NOT touched; this is additive.
_meta.time_assumption: Assumes 'now' is calendar 2026 so year-1 = 2025 is the current complete year.
_meta.engineering: Baseline All-Residential homes_sold 2022=9000, 2023=8500, 2024=8000 (mean 8500, population std ~408). Current 2025=7000 -> z = (7000-8500)/408 ~= -3.67 (clearly bearish, z <= -1.0). Non-headline rows (Condo 99999, SFR 4800) MUST be filtered out — if the 99999 leaked in, current-year velocity would invert to bullish, so the property-type filter is load-bearing for the bearish read.
